tasks.title: A `.team/runbooks/add-cli-command.yml` runbook exists with a valid schema and at least 4 tasks.
A `.team/runbooks/add-github-integration.yml` runbook exists with a valid schema and at least 4 tasks.
A `.team/runbooks/add-test-suite.yml` runbook exists with a valid schema and at least 4 tasks.
`matchRunbook(description, runbooksDir)` returns the best-matching runbook or `null` when score is below threshold.
Guard runbook tests from approval-gate and buffer overflow
tasks.description: Audit test/runbook-match.test.mjs to confirm it imports only from bin/lib/runbook.mjs and test fixture YAMLs — no imports that pull in the approval-gate, GitHub polling, or run.mjs integration paths. If any transitive import triggers the gate, stub or mock it. Also verify the test file is registered in package.json test glob without duplicates that could cause the test runner to emit excessive output. Run the single file in isolation with `node --test test/runbook-match.test.mjs` and confirm it exits cleanly with no ENOBUFS or approval-gate output.

tasks.title: Bypass approval-gate when NODE_ENV=test
tasks.description: In the approval-gate module (or wherever the polling loop is started), add a guard: if process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test' (or the TEST env var is set), skip issue creation and polling entirely and resolve immediately. Also ensure the test npm script sets NODE_ENV=test so every test file benefits automatically. This eliminates the ENOBUFS buffer-overflow risk by preventing the large polling stdout from being captured during test runs.

tasks.title: Add NODE_ENV=test short-circuit to approval-gate before any polling
tasks.description: In the approval-gate module, check process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test' (or SKIP_APPROVAL_GATE env var) at the top of the wait/poll function and return immediately with an approved result. Ensure no GitHub API calls or issue creation are attempted when running under test. Verify the existing integrity-check warning path also short-circuits cleanly.

tasks.title: Fix spawnSync ENOBUFS in outer-loop by increasing maxBuffer or switching to streaming
tasks.description: The spawnSync call accumulating stdout hits Node's default 1 MB buffer limit. Either pass maxBuffer: 10 * 1024 * 1024 to all spawnSync/execSync calls in outer-loop.mjs and related test helpers, or switch to spawn with piped streams. Also ensure test fixtures do not emit unbounded output (e.g. suppress polling log lines when NODE_ENV=test).

tasks.title: Guard runbook tests from approval-gate and buffer overflow
tasks.description: Audit test/runbook-match.test.mjs to confirm it imports only from bin/lib/runbook.mjs and test fixture YAMLs — no imports that pull in the approval-gate, GitHub polling, or run.mjs integration paths. If any transitive import triggers the gate, stub or mock it. Also verify the test file is registered in package.json test glob without duplicates that could cause the test runner to emit excessive output. Run the single file in isolation with `node --test test/runbook-match.test.mjs` and confirm it exits cleanly with no ENOBUFS or approval-gate output.

tasks.title: Prevent approval-gate from triggering during npm test
tasks.description: Add a guard in the approval-gate and outer-loop code so that when NODE_TEST_CONTEXT or a TEST env var is set (as it is under node --test), the approval polling path is skipped entirely. This stops the test suite from blocking on issue #42 and eliminates the flood of polling output that causes the buffer overflow.

tasks.title: Guard runbook tests from buffer overflow and approval-gate side-effects
tasks.description: With the approval-gate guard in place, re-attempt the original task: ensure test/runbook-match.test.mjs and any outer-loop tests that spawn subprocesses use maxBuffer-safe options or stream-based child_process calls (exec/spawn async) instead of spawnSync, so ENOBUFS cannot recur even if output grows.

tasks.title: `matchRunbook(description, runbooksDir)` returns the best-matching runbook or `null` when score is below threshold.
Regex match in a runbook correctly narrows candidate set before keyword scoring.
`planTasks()` uses runbook tasks when a match is found, falling through to brainstorm when no match.
`agt run --runbook add-cli-command` forces that runbook regardless of feature description.
`agt run --runbook nonexistent` logs a warning and falls through to brainstorm.
Console output identifies the matched runbook name and score before task dispatch begins.
`include:` in a task entry is resolved one level deep and inline tasks are inserted at that position.
Unit tests cover: exact regex match, keyword-only match, no-match fallthrough, `--runbook` override, unknown runbook fallthrough, `include:` resolution.
Existing tests pass without modification (no regression).
`test/runbooks.test.mjs` passes with ≥11 cases covering all acceptance criteria scenarios.
3 built-in runbooks exist in `.team/runbooks/` with valid schema and ≥4 tasks each.
`planTasks()` in `run.mjs` uses runbook tasks on match and falls through correctly on no-match.
`agt run --runbook <name>` flag parsed and wired to `planTasks()`.
Full test suite (`npm test`) passes with no regressions.
Console output names the matched runbook before task execution begins.
